title: Ethical Behaviour and the Awakening are one
justification: This talk directly addresses the chapter theme by establishing the inseparable unity of ethical conduct (sila) and wisdom (pañña), providing the philosophical foundation for Buddhist ethics. It offers rich examples (Angulimala story, TV exercise, Costa Coffee), clear explanations of key terms (kusala, punya), and flows naturally from theory to practical application. The talk has strong literary qualities with memorable analogies and a coherent structure that can serve as a spine for grafting complementary material on precepts, perfections, and daily practice.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma explains that ethical behavior and awakening are inseparable—understanding naturally manifests as right conduct, and right conduct deepens understanding. Through the Eightfold Path framework and vivid examples, he demonstrates how virtue is both skillful and an end in itself, with merit (punya) arising naturally from wholesome action.

relevance.note: Directly about unity of wisdom and ethical conduct
